mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically increasing present valuations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 6.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, boosting corporate earnings expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 6.0 within a 3-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in industrial production and housing starts indicate broad-based economic acceleration, which expands profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative or zero directional accuracy below 50% over a 3-year out-of-sample test, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression yields a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: In a rising inflation environment, a 1-month increase in the CPI index exceeding 0.5 percent forces market expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the coefficient on CPI momentum in a predictive regression remains statistically insignificant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sequential decline in housing starts signals weakening aggregate demand and reduced construction investment, which lowers corporate revenue forecasts and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns show no statistically significant negative skew during consecutive monthly declines in HOUST, or the signal's information ratio falls below 0.2 over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.4 percentage points over a single month, it signals a sudden labor market deterioration that triggers recession fears, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 45% of historical instances where UNRATE jumps by >0.4pp, or the out-of-sample Sharpe ratio of a long-only strategy based on this trigger exceeds 0.5.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where real personal savings decline while the federal funds rate remains elevated drains consumer liquidity and increases borrowing costs simultaneously, suppressing equity valuations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS momentum and FEDFUNDS levels yields a positive coefficient in a forward-return regression, or the joint signal's directional accuracy drops below 48% over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of supply chain stress, a month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 1.0 percent combined with stagnant housing starts indicates structural bottlenecks that erode corporate margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances where INDPRO drops >1.0% while HOUST remains flat, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for VIX levels.
